name: ManageEngine IT Management Suite API
what_it_does: Comprehensive IT management suite with REST APIs — providing ITSM (ServiceDesk Plus), endpoint management (Desktop Central/Endpoint Central), network monitoring (OpManager), Active Directory management, patch management, and privileged access management across 120+ IT management products for SMB and enterprise IT teams.
use_cases: Creating and managing IT service desk tickets via ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus API
Querying endpoint health and patch compliance data from Desktop Central via API
Integrating ManageEngine with monitoring tools for automated incident creation
Managing Active Directory users, groups, and policies via AD Manager Plus API
Pulling network topology and device health data from OpManager for NOC dashboards
not_for: Enterprise-scale ITSM requiring deep enterprise features (use ServiceNow for large enterprise)
Marketing or CRM software (ManageEngine focuses exclusively on IT operations)
Pure SaaS-only organizations without on-premises IT infrastructure
best_when: An IT team managing Windows-heavy environments needs affordable, comprehensive IT management tools covering helpdesk, endpoint, network, and Active Directory — at a fraction of ServiceNow or BMC Helix pricing.
avoid_when: You're a cloud-native organization without on-premises infrastructure, need enterprise ITSM scale, or prefer modern SaaS-native tooling over traditional IT management platforms.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code flow for cloud products. API key (technician key) for on-premises ServiceDesk Plus. Bearer token auth. Product-scoped access (ServiceDesk, Desktop Central, OpManager each have separate APIs). Webhook for ticket state changes. Zoho (parent company) OAuth for Zoho ecosystem integration. Each product has separate API documentation. On-premises products use technician key auth; cloud products use OAuth2.

pricing.free_tier_limits: ServiceDesk Plus free: up to 5 technicians; limited features
pricing.paid_tiers: ServiceDesk Plus Standard from $10/technician/month; Enterprise from $50/technician/month
pricing.notes: Indian company (Chennai). Founded 1996 as AdventNet, became ManageEngine brand. Wholly owned by Zoho Corporation. 280,000+ organizations use ManageEngine products. 120+ products covering full IT management stack. Zoho ecosystem integration. Strong in Windows-heavy environments. Significantly more affordable than ServiceNow/BMC for mid-market. SolarWinds competitor for network management. Endpoint Central (formerly Desktop Central) is market leader in mid-market endpoint management.

performance.rate_limits: Product-specific rate limits; not publicly documented for all products

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST APIs for each product (ServiceDesk Plus, Desktop Central, OpManager, ADManager Plus, etc.). Different auth per product (technician key vs OAuth2). Moderate documentation quality — varies by product. 120+ products means fragmented API landscape. On-premises vs cloud versions have different API configurations. Webhook for ITSM events. Zoho integration for cloud products.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Ticket reads fully idempotent. Asset reads idempotent. Ticket creation not idempotent — use ticket ID for updates. On-premises vs cloud API behavior may differ.
known_agent_gotchas: 120+ products — each with separate API, documentation, and auth; verify which product's API you need
On-premises (technician key auth) vs cloud (OAuth2) have different auth flows — clarify deployment type
Indian company — US/EU data residency available; verify data processing agreements
ManageEngine 2022 security incidents — verify current security posture before deployment
Zoho ecosystem integration available but requires Zoho account and OAuth configuration

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. Multi-region data residency. OAuth2 and API key auth. Indian company with notable 2022 security incidents (CVE vulnerabilities exploited in wild) — apply patches promptly. On-premises deployments require internal security hardening. Active Directory integration gives elevated access to identity infrastructure — strict access control required. Privileged access manager product handles credentials — implement zero-trust for PAM API.
